summary: This week saw elevated DeFi security activity with two critical incidents involving bridge protocols and several high-severity exploits targeting DEX platforms and lending protocols. Flash loan attacks and oracle manipulation remain the most prevalent attack vectors.

topThreats.name: Bridge Protocol Exploits
topThreats.description: Two critical bridge vulnerabilities were identified, including a relay mechanism exploit draining $12M and a multisig key compromise threatening $8M in locked assets.

topThreats.name: Flash Loan & Oracle Attacks
topThreats.description: Multiple incidents of flash loan-powered oracle manipulation targeting AMMs and governance systems, with combined losses exceeding $4.7M.

topThreats.name: Front-End Compromises
topThreats.description: A DEX aggregator suffered a front-end injection attack redirecting user approvals, highlighting growing supply chain risks in DeFi interfaces.

trendAnalysis: Bridge protocols continue to be high-value targets, with attack sophistication increasing from simple exploit patterns to multi-vector approaches combining key compromise with social engineering. The emergence of governance attacks via flash loans signals a concerning new trend that protocols with on-chain voting must address urgently.
recommendations: Audit all cross-chain bridge relay mechanisms for unvalidated external calls and implement defense-in-depth validation
Migrate from TWAP-based on-chain oracles to decentralized oracle networks like Chainlink for critical price feeds
Implement time-locked governance proposals with snapshot-based voting to prevent flash loan governance attacks
Deploy real-time front-end integrity monitoring and enforce strict Content Security Policies on all DeFi interfaces
Increase multisig threshold requirements for bridge operations and enforce hardware security module usage for key management
